**Web Engineering Project**

**Goal:**

- Create a web application of your choice as long as it meets the technical requirements.

**Technical Requirements:**

1. Online and offline support.
2. Have authentication.
3. At least 3 entities in the data model.
4. Must include at least one real-time feature using Web Sockets.
5. Have a feature that requires a cron job.
6. 90+ in all Lighthouse Report Metrics in both desktop and mobile.
7. Responsive in both desktop and mobile.

**Restrictions**

1. Do not use a third party library that would remove the need of a backend application layer. Example: Supabase JS
2. It should have separate frontend application and backend application.

_You may use libraries this time._

**Graded Tasks:**

1. **Project proposal.**  
   Submit a project proposal that should include the following:
   1. Design document that will explain the design of your frontend and backend application. It can be a flow chart or whatever you decide is easier to explain your project. It is important that it will explain the details of technical implementation so I can comment before you will implement it with code. It may include the following: the database schema, planned endpoints, cron jobs, connection of front and back, tech stack, etc.  
      ![][image1]  
      ![][image2]
   2. Low-fidelity UI design. It is important that you show here the layout for desktop and mobile per screen needed. The goal of this design is for me to understand how many screens you are going to create and help me understand your features.  
      ![][image3]

   Deadline: April 13 and 16, 2026 (Lab Time)

   Points: 30pts Design Document, 20 pts for UI Design

2. **Weekly progress report.**  
   Submit a weekly progress report that will contain things you have done, things to do for next week, and blockers. For things that are done and things to do, specify which group member is assigned or responsible for.

   Deadline: April 24, May 1, May 8, 2026

   Points: 20pts per week.

3. **Web Application.**  
   Add me as a collaborator to your GitHub repository. Submit a Pull request.  
   Points are based on code review feedback.
   Deadline: May 16, 2026  
   Points: 100pts  

4. **Project Presentation.**
   Deadline: May 16, 2026 (Saturday) \- to be discussed with the class.
   Points: 100pts.  

5. **Peer Evaluation**  
   Points: 30pts

**Deadlines:**

- April 13, 2026: Project Proposal
- April 24, May 1, May 8: Weekly Progress Report
- May 11 and 14: Project Presentation
- May 21, 2026: Peer Evaluation
